b:T2c15c,<div class="specification">
<p>The micrograph shows a section through part of the liver. The diagram illustrates the details of what is found within the marked area.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><strong>[micrograph]</strong> Micrograph of liver. https://undergraduate.vetmed.wsu.edu/courses/vph-308/histology/lab-1-histologycells-<br/>and-organelles/liver-slide-wsu_2_052 courtesy of Patrick D. Wilson, MS, DVM, Clinical Associate Professor,<br/>Veterinary Integrative Biosciences, College of Veterinary Medicine &amp; Biosciences, Texas A&amp;M University.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><strong>[diagram]</strong> Reprinted by permission from Springer Nature, <em>Nature Reviews Immunology</em>, “Aberrant homing of mucosal<br/>T cells and extra-intestinal manifestations of inflammatory bowel disease” by Adams and Eksteen ©2006. <a href="https://www.nature.com/nri/">https://www.nature.com/nri/</a>. </p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Identify the phagocyte labelled X.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Identify the blood vessel labelled Y which brings blood from the intestines.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Explain the function of hepatocytes in protein metabolism.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Compare and contrast the structure of sinusoids and capillaries.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/d/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","d"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"20N.3.SL.TZ0.18"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","3"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"20N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","d-3-functions-of-the-liver"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Kupffer (cell);</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.i.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>(hepatic) portal vein/venule;</p>\n<p><em>“Portal” alone is insufficient</em></p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.ii.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>a. they produce/secrete <span style=\"text-decoration: underline;\">plasma</span> proteins;</p>\n<p>b. (the plasma proteins) are modified/secreted by the Golgi apparatus;</p>\n<p>c. protein/globin is broken down into amino acids;</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>a. both have walls 1 cell thick/same thickness<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>both have a basement membrane;</p>\n<p>b. sinusoids have pores/holes/fenestrations<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>sinusoids have a wider lumen;</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">c.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Most candidates could identify the Kupffer cell (with a variety of spellings), and the hepatic portal vein.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.i.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.ii.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>The function of the hepatocytes in protein metabolism was challenging for most candidates despite this being taken directly from an assessment statement.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Comparison of sinusoids and capillaries produced many vague answers. Many candidates failed to treat the question as a ‘compare and contrast’ question which requires giving similarities and differences. Common errors included referring to blood type carried, cell membrane thickness and the position of the vessels in the body.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">c.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
